description: A designed vertical 'headline/news-quote' graphic combining a hero photo of President Donald Trump with a press excerpt. The top two-thirds is a photograph of Trump from the chest up, wearing a navy suit, white shirt, red tie, and an American-flag lapel pin, looking slightly upward/off-camera against a softly blurred bokeh crowd background (apparent stadium/event). Across the lower third, on a dark navy panel, a white italic-and-bold serif headline reads 'Putting Americans FIRST!'. Below it sits a white rounded text box containing the masthead of 'The Dallas Express' (Old English/blackletter logo) followed by a quoted article passage: 'Under President Donald Trump, the Labor Department has begun using an authority that lawyers reportedly said had gone largely untouched for years: secretary-certified investigations that can proceed without a formal worker complaint, so long as officials claim there is reasonable cause to believe an employer may be breaking the law.' The design technique pairs a flattering presidential portrait with a favorable third-party news quotation to lend credibility to DOL's 'Project Firewall' enforcement push against employers accused of prioritizing foreign labor over Americans. Target audience: general public and the administration's base, framing aggressive labor enforcement as pro-American-worker.
notable_text: Putting Americans FIRST!; The Dallas Express; secretary-certified investigations that can proceed without a formal worker complaint... reasonable cause to believe an employer may be breaking the law.
